patch.value:
## Context
Git-native storage is a hard constraint (ADR pm-i7i4: the filesystem is the database; ADR pm-bl8x decision driver: everything must remain "diffable, mergeable, and recoverable from the working tree alone"). But pm has never defined what "mergeable" actually means when two branches mutate the same item: worktree agents branch from main today, and at thousands of concurrent agents, branch-and-merge becomes a primary workflow, not an edge case.

## Problem
- Two branches editing the same `.toon` item file produce raw git conflicts with no field-aware resolution guidance (last-writer-wins on a whole file loses the other branch's fields).
- History JSONL streams are hash-chained (appendLineAtomic anchoring): a naive union merge of two diverged `history/<id>.jsonl` files breaks chain integrity; `pm history-repair` (shipped in pm-85hm) can re-anchor drift but is not merge-aware and must be invoked manually per item.
- There is no post-merge verification story: `pm validate` / `pm health` do not flag "this workspace was just merged and N history chains diverged / M items have conflict markers".

## Deliverables (scope for grooming)
1. Documented merge semantics per artifact class: item `.toon` (field-level three-way guidance or documented ours/theirs policy), history `.jsonl` (union + mandatory re-anchor), locks/runtime (ignore), search index (rebuild).
2. `.gitattributes` recommendations and, if justified, a custom git merge driver shipped as a pm package (check existing unbraind packages first — none cover this as of 2026-07-07 audit #16).
3. Post-merge reconciliation entrypoint: e.g. `pm validate --post-merge` advisory or doctor check that detects conflict markers, diverged chains, and duplicate IDs from parallel branch creation (relates to pm-khdq ID TOCTOU), with remediation hints wiring into history-repair.
4. Docs: worktree/branch agent workflow guidance (AGENT_GUIDE) covering when to branch tracker data vs. mutate main.

## Relationships
- pm-is1a (multi-workspace federation) is the superset problem; single-workspace multi-branch merge is the prerequisite and should land first.
- SDK-first (pm-usfg): reconciliation logic lands as an SDK primitive; CLI is a shim.
- Horizon: long (with pm-9rxu scale-out); design/ADR input can start earlier.

## Duplicate check (2026-07-07 audit #16)
Searched "merge conflict branch tracker", "merge driver git worktree branches", "post-merge reconcile history chain" — only history-repair (pm-85hm, shipped, drift-only) and consolidation item pm-tqel matched; no existing item covers merge semantics or post-merge reconciliation.
Tracker merge semantics: field-aware merge guidance, history-chain-safe JSONL merging, and post-merge reconciliation for multi-branch agent workflows

Define and tool what 'mergeable' means for .agents/pm data when branches/worktrees mutate the same items: per-artifact merge semantics (.toon items, hash-chained history JSONL, runtime/index), .gitattributes or merge-driver guidance, and a post-merge validate/doctor advisory that detects conflict markers, diverged history chains, and duplicate IDs with remediation hints.

Documented merge semantics for every tracker artifact class; post-merge detection surfaced via validate or health with remediation hints wiring into history-repair; worktree/branch agent workflow documented in AGENT_GUIDE; any merge tooling ships SDK-first with the CLI as a shim

author: maintainer-agent
message: Graph pass #92: history-chain-safe JSONL merge guidance in g5sx builds directly on the append-only history stream contract klo8 owns

author: maintainer-agent
message: Graph pass #92: field-aware merge semantics are the conflict-resolution half of cross-workspace federation

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-19 intake wave. Maintainer dogfood issues GH-595..612 landed a verified multi-agent branch-merge defect matrix; the artifact-class children now under this feature: pm-wc1r (GH-599 history jsonl driver, Feature P1), pm-9q2t (GH-606 .toon count-header appends, P1), pm-m3nl (GH-608 updated_at scalar conflicts), pm-wwfd (GH-611 delete/modify resurrection, P1), pm-xdn6 (GH-609 config/schema layer), pm-gpo7 (GH-603 history-repair cements loss, P1), pm-ol3p (GH-604 verify strict-exit gate). Outside this parent but in the family: pm-cxyv (GH-607 validate skips unparseable items, under pm-33cw), pm-pibw (GH-600 cross-branch id collisions, under pm-dj98), pm-4uqm (GH-598 gitignore hardcoded root), pm-xh82 (GH-612 additive AC). Suggested execution order: cxyv+ol3p (gates first — make corruption visible), then wc1r+9q2t+m3nl (drivers), then wwfd+xdn6 (policies), gpo7 rides the wc1r+ol3p loop. GH-610 closed as dup of GH-590 (shipped PR#593).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Family-map addition 2026-07-19: NEW artifact class - SDK workspace-transaction journals (<pmRoot>/transactions/sdk/<txid>.json, shipped PR#605). Per-branch runtime receipts that conflict on every concurrent merge if the repo tracks .agents/pm; not covered by the init gitignore block and never GC'd. Owned by new pm-8xod (parent usfg, related here) - gitignore-block coverage + retention/GC + tracked-repo merge disposition. No change to the execution order gates (cxyv, ol3p first).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
2026-07-19 reconciliation (post-PR#614): the entire first merge-safety wave SHIPPED — all seven children closed (wc1r history JSONL union/reanchor driver, 9q2t field-aware .toon item driver, m3nl updated_at latest-timestamp resolution, xdn6 JSON config/schema key-level driver, cxyv validate --check-storage-integrity, ol3p history --verify --strict-exit, gpo7 cross-author reconciliation reports in repair) plus 4uqm custom-root gitignore and 8xod transactions GC (gc --scope transactions). GH-598/599/603/604/606/607/608/609/611 all closed. Execution order held: gates → drivers → policies. Public SDK primitives: field-aware item merge, history union/reanchor, recursive JSON merge; installer via `pm merge install` (idempotent fenced .gitattributes + local driver config, real %P paths). Docs: MERGE_SAFETY.md.

Remaining frontier under this umbrella: pm-iwsj (adopt the drivers + CI integrity/verify gates in the pm-cli repo itself — migration steps from PR#614 are NOT yet applied here: no .gitattributes fence, no CI storage-integrity/strict-exit step), pm-pibw (cross-branch id collisions, parent dj98), and any policy-layer work (merge policy selection per artifact class) stays extend-here, never new umbrellas.

value.note: Multi-branch/worktree workflow section closing the AGENT_GUIDE AC half

author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: Delivered the complete tracker merge-semantics program: field-aware merge drivers for every artifact class (items, history, relationships, config/schema, transactions), committed .gitattributes fence with drift audit and schema-triggered refresh, post-merge detection via validate --check-storage-integrity and history --verify --strict-exit with remediation wiring into history-repair, cross-branch id collision controls (configurable ids.token_length + duplicate detection), SDK-first implementation under sdk/merge with the CLI as a shim, and MERGE_SAFETY.md plus AGENT_GUIDE multi-branch workflow documentation.
Multi-agent branch/worktree workflows merge tracker artifacts without conflicts or silent corruption, with documented semantics and automated post-merge verification.

Shipped across PR#593/#594/#605/#614/#616/#617: merge drivers + fence + union/renumbering live, CI runs storage-integrity and strict history verification, MERGE_SAFETY.md documents every artifact class, AGENT_GUIDE routes agents into the workflow; nightly and CI green post-adoption.

Delivered the complete tracker merge-semantics program: field-aware merge drivers for every artifact class (items, history, relationships, config/schema, transactions), committed .gitattributes fence with drift audit and schema-triggered refresh, post-merge detection via validate --check-storage-integrity and history --verify --strict-exit with remediation wiring into history-repair, cross-branch id collision controls (configurable ids.token_length + duplicate detection), SDK-first implementation under sdk/merge with the CLI as a shim, and MERGE_SAFETY.md plus AGENT_GUIDE multi-branch workflow documentation.

message: All four AC halves verified shipped across the PR#593/#594/#605/#614/#616/#617 wave; AGENT_GUIDE multi-branch section added as the final documentation half.
